steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says the book “has earned a lot of praise,” and “In fact” signals the next sentence amplifies that idea. The blank needs a verb meaning to obtain or be given a recognition or award.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — guessed: to guess means to estimate without certainty — you don’t guess an award, and the word carries no sense of receiving recognition. ✗
  • Choice B — blamed: means to hold responsible for something negative — the opposite of the positive context. ✗
  • Choice C — received: means to be given something; “received the award” is natural, precise, and continues the idea of earned praise. ✓
  • Choice D — forgot: implies the subject lost memory of something — logically impossible for a book to forget an award and contradicts the positive tone. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
